analysis.summary: The technician upgraded the Cisco Secure Client, specifically its AnyConnect VPN and Umbrella modules, to version 5.1.16.194. This involved listing existing installations, copying new installer files, running the installer with specific module selections, and verifying the successful upgrade via PowerShell, followed by cleanup of installation files.
actions_taken: Used PowerShell to identify currently installed Cisco Secure Client components and their versions.
Copied new Cisco Secure Client installer files (version 5.1.16.194) to a temporary directory C:\cisco.
Launched the Cisco Secure Client installer application.
Selected 'Umbrella' and 'Diagnostic And Reporting Tool' modules for installation/upgrade.
Accepted the Cisco Secure Client End User License Agreement (EULA).
Initiated the installation, which included removing older versions of Cisco Secure Client AnyConnect VPN and Umbrella.
Verified the successful upgrade of Cisco Secure Client AnyConnect VPN and Umbrella to version 5.1.16.194 using PowerShell.
Deleted the temporary installation files from C:\cisco.
applications_used: Cisco Secure Client Install Selector (Installer Application)
issues_observed: Older versions of Cisco Secure Client - AnyConnect VPN (5.1.15.287) and Cisco Secure Client - Umbrella (5.1.15.287) were present on the system.
issues_resolved: Cisco Secure Client - AnyConnect VPN was successfully upgraded to version 5.1.16.194.
Cisco Secure Client - Umbrella was successfully upgraded to version 5.1.16.194.
notable_events: The Cisco Secure Client installer automatically processed the AnyConnect VPN module upgrade even when 'Umbrella' was the primary selected module for installation.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician worked methodically, first assessing the existing software, then staging the new installation, performing the upgrade, and finally verifying the results. There was a brief moment of adjusting the module selection in the installer, but this was quickly corrected.
coaching_flags: While effective, the technician initially selected all modules in the installer before deselecting to only 'Umbrella' and 'Diagnostic And Reporting Tool'. A quick confirmation of required modules upfront could streamline this step, though the correction was made promptly.
